content.rendered:
[vc_row triangle_shape=”no”][vc_column width=”1/2″][vc_gallery type=”image_grid” images=”1012,204,208,207,206,616,212,614,612,659″ img_size=”medium” onclick=””][vc_empty_space][/vc_column][vc_column width=”1/2″][vc_column_text]

Chuck T. Murphy has been a member of the Rotary Club and Chamber of Commerce for over 30 years.  He is the past president of the Thousand Oaks Rotary Club and has served on its Board of Directors. The following is a list of volunteer and non-profit projects that he has been involved in:

  • North Hollywood Rotary Club
  • Thousand Oaks Rotary Club
  • North Hollywood Chamber of Commerce
  • Conejo Valley Chamber of Commerce

The following is a list of awards/certificates that Chuck T. Murphy, Esq. has received over the years through his dedication to improving the community, whether at the local, city, state, national, or international level:

  • City of Fresno Special City Council Resolution honoring Mr. Murphy for his outstanding community service work
  • Certificate of Appreciation from the California State Senate
  • Certificate of Recognition from the California State Assembly
  • Certificate of Special Congressional Recognition
  • MVP Award by the North Hollywood Chamber of Commerce
  • 2014 Special Service Award by the North Hollywood Chamber of Commerce
  • Paul Harris Fellow Award
  • Family & Community Service Award
  • Rotarian of the Year Award
  • Mike O-Beirne Award
  • Special Recognition for Group Study Exchange Program by the Thousand Oaks Rotary Club

Mr. Murphy has also contributed hundreds of hours of pro bono work towards setting up non-profit organizations that have helped communities local and worldwide.  The following is a list of the non-profit organizations he helped form:

  • East Valley Chapter of the American
  • San Fernando Valley Charitable Foundation
  • Campe de Cahuenga Historical Memorial Association
  • Friends of Toluca Lake School
  • Tarzana Encino Rotary Club Charitable Foundation
  • Autism Pins, Inc.
  • Canyon Oaks Sportman’s Club
  • Communities United for Smart Growth
  • The GIP Foundation
  • Michael Hoefflin Foundation
  • Hollywood High School Alumni Association
  • Scholastic Performance Foundation

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row triangle_shape=”no”][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row]
